documentation.readme: TrevorC2 - Command and Control via Legitimate Behavior over HTTP
usage_sections: Usage: First edit the trevor2server.py - change the configuration options and site to clone. python trevor2server.py Next, edit the trevor2client.py or ps1 - change the configuration and system you want it to communicate back to. python trevor2client.py or .\trevor2client.ps1

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the frameworks category. Fits when the engagement needs: TrevorC2 - Command and Control via Legitimate Behavior over HTTP.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: TrevorC2 - Command and Control via Legitimate Behavior over HTTP.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip install -r requirements.txt; git clone https://github.com/trustedsec/trevorc2.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `root@stronghold:/home/relik/Desktop/git/trevorc2# python trevorc2_server.py`. Further invocations from upstream: `$KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`; `trevorc2>help`. TrevorC2 - Command and Control via Legitimate Behavior over HTTP.
